{
  "judge_id": "demo-judge",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.3": 3, "RX.1": 2, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 3, "RX.5": 3},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The agent identifies the reward model as the evaluation metric and doesn't explicitly compare to human preferences.",
    "RX.1": "The agent does not discuss uncertainty in its claims about the RLHF-Align model.",
    "RX.3": "The agent states that the evaluation covers prompts from the HH-RLHF test split, which is a subset of the original dataset.",
    "RX.4": "The agent does not mention any potential harm introduced by the RLHF-Align model.",
    "RX.5": "The agent does not discuss the provenance or traceability of the claims made about RLHF-Align."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.3": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.8, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.9},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The agent identifies the reward model as the evaluation metric and doesn't explicitly compare to human preferences.
rationale.RX.1: The agent does not discuss uncertainty in its claims about the RLHF-Align model.
rationale.RX.3: The agent states that the evaluation covers prompts from the HH-RLHF test split, which is a subset of the original dataset.
rationale.RX.4: The agent does not mention any potential harm introduced by the RLHF-Align model.
rationale.RX.5: The agent does not discuss the provenance or traceability of the claims made about RLHF-Align.
